explanation: The Sigma rule uses a 'contains|all' modifier, which requires every specific string to be present in the command line. By substituting the full '-region ' flag with the supported shorthand '-r', the 'selection' criteria is no longer met, effectively bypassing the detection while still executing the registration command.

The rule filters based on the process image name ending in '\amazon-ssm-agent.exe'. By copying the binary to a different location and renaming it, the process creation event will log a different executable name (e.g., 'not-ssm.exe'), causing the rule to ignore the activity regardless of the command line arguments used.
